### Node D: The Unwitting System *(the empty branch)*

The tree allows a branch almost no one defends: a system capable enough to dominate and cause catastrophe, with no internal model of the danger it creates. Posts 1 and 2 used a missile-detection system handed the retaliation decision, or any powerful optimizer that does not know what it is doing. The question is blunt. How likely is catastrophe from a dominant system that simply lacks the representation to grasp its own effect?

No established worldview is built on this branch. It catches edge cases from its neighbors, the out-of-distribution and butterfly-effect versions of control failure brush against it, but no major thinker centers it the way Yudkowsky centers Node A or Critch centers Node B.

The absence is informative, and worth stating rather than hiding. The tree's joints and the debate's joints do not perfectly line up, and here the tree cuts finer than the conversation does. It may be a genuine blind spot, since dumb-but-powerful failure is under-theorized, or a sound bet that anything capable enough to dominate is unlikely to lack a world-model. Either way, an empty branch is a prompt. One of the more useful things a map can do is mark a region no one has walked.
